m_Item1.QuestionText: CSS stands for __________
m_Item1.Explanation: CSS is a style sheet language which stands for Cascading Style Sheet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In CSS, h1 can be called as _______
m_Item1.Explanation: HTML element h1 is used in CSS for styling, then it is also called a selector. Attributes are the special words which control the element’s behaviour. To show the start and end of HTML element, tag is used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In css, “color:red” can be called as _____________
m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, color:red is the declaration for an element saying that the given element has to apply a red color. Color is one of CSS property.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following attributes is used to specify elements to bind style rules to?
m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, styling elements can be done by id, class and tag attribute.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which selector is used to specify a rule to bind a particular unique element?
m_Item1.Explanation: For binding a particular unique element, id selectors are used. While for group of elements, class selector can be used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In CSS, “font-size” can be called as ________
m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, font-size is a property which increases/decreases the font of text.

m_Item1.QuestionText: _________ selectors are used to select multiple elements.
m_Item1.Explanation: Class selectors are used to specify a group of elements. Id selector specifies a particular unique element. Tag selector even will allow to select all elements having a particular tag name.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following tag is used to embed css in html page?
m_Item1.Explanation: <style> </style> tag is used to embed CSS in HTML page, while <script> </script> is used to embed JS in HTML. <!DOCTYPE html> is HTML5 declaration.

m_Item1.QuestionText: __________ has introduced text, list, box, margin, border, color, and background properties.
m_Item1.Explanation: CSS is a style sheet language which stands for Cascading Style Sheet. CSS has introduced text, list, box, margin, border, color, and background properties.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Is it the best way to include H1 heading only one time on the web page.
m_Item1.Explanation: Yes, including an H1 heading only one time on a webpage is the best practice. For subheadings, you can leverage H2 tags for main sections, H3 tags for subsections, and so on, creating a well-organized structure.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is the CSS Extension Prefixes for Webkit?
m_Item1.Explanation: Browser sometimes adds prefixes to non-standard CSS properties. CSS Extension prefix for Webkit is -webkit which is supported by almost all ios browsers. -o is used by opera where as -moz is used by firefox browser.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS property is equivalent for the element <center>?
m_Item1.Explanation: The element <center> in html aligns the text in center. In CSS, this is equivalent to text-align property which can align text to right, left or center as per the value. Margin property sets the margin for an element. Color property is used to set/assign colour to element. Font property is for increasing/decreasing the font of text.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS property is Equivalent for the align attribute?
m_Item1.Explanation: In the case of some elements such as <img>, the CSS float property is more appropriate than text-align. text-align. Center attribute can only align text to center. Now-a-days it is not supported.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is the latest version of CSS available?
m_Item1.Explanation: There are three different kinds of versions of CSS i.e CSS1, CSS2 and CSS3. Out of them CSS3 is the latest version to be used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS property is equivalent for the element <font>?
m_Item1.Explanation: Both font-style and font-family are for styling the text. They show text in different kinds of styles. Color is for setting/assigning colour to text or element. The font-size property in CSS is equivalent to attribute <font>. Both of them either increase/decrease the font of text. <font> element is deprecated now.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following CSS property is used to give a line over the text?
m_Item1.Explanation:
• Text-decoration is a CSS property used to decorate the text.
  • The text-decoration property takes many values such as overline, underline, line-through, none, inherit, etc.
  • Overline gives a line over the text. Underline value is used to add line under the text.
  • None value defines a normal text.
  • When one wants to give a line through the text, line-through value for text-property can be used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The first CSS specification to become an official W3C Recommendation is ___________
m_Item1.Explanation: The first CSS specification to become an official W3C Recommendation is CSS level 1, published on December 17, 1996. Håkon Wium Lie and Bert Bos are credited as the original developers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: There is no single, integrated ____________________ because it is split into separate modules.
m_Item1.Explanation: Here is no single, integrated CSS4 specification,[42] because it is split into separate modules. However, there are “level 4” modules.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct HTML for referring to an external style sheet?
m_Item1.Explanation: To refer an external style sheet : <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="mystyle.css"/>

m_Item1.QuestionText: The CSS padding property is used for?
m_Item1.Explanation: CSS padding property is used to provide space between element's content and its border.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The default value of "position" attribute is _________.
m_Item1.Explanation: static is a default value of position attribute.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How will you make all paragraph elements 'RED' in color ?
m_Item1.Explanation: syntax: tag name { attributename:value;}  to apply style to any element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: By default Hyperlinks are displayed with an underline. How do you remove the underline from all hyperlinks by using CSS code ?
m_Item1.Explanation: To remove underline from any work or hyperlinks use a {text-decoration:none;}

m_Item1.QuestionText: “font-style comes first than font-weight in font attribute”. State true or false.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Syntax:
font: font-style font-variant font-weight font-size/line-height font-family is the sequence in which it needs to be written.  The font-size and font-family values are required.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following CSS property defines the different properties of all four sides of an element’s border in a single declaration?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border property is used as a shortcut which sets different properties of an element’s four borders. e.g. border: 2px solid blue

m_Item1.QuestionText: Identify the CSS property defining bottom-left corner shape of the border?
m_Item1.Explanation: CSS3 border-bottom-left-radius property allows to give “rounded corners” for specified corner of the table.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Select the CSS property that sets the width of an element’s bottom border?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border-bottom-width CSS property sets the width of the bottom border of a box.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following CSS property sets the color of an element’s four borders?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border-color property sets the color of an element’s four borders. This property can have from one to four values.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Choose the CSS property that can be used for collapsing the borders between table cells?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border-collapse property sets whether the table borders are collapsed into a single border or detached as in standard HTML.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS property can be used to set the image as border instead of the border style?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border-image-source property specifies the path to the image to be used as a border (instead of the normal border around an element).

m_Item1.QuestionText: In how many way border-image-width CSS property can be defined?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border-image-width CSS property can have single value, two values, three values or four values very similar like padding and margin property.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not an appropriate value for border-collapse?
m_Item1.Explanation: Except option a, all other values can be set to border collapse CSS property.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property defines the border-width, border-style and border-color of an element’s top border in a single declaration?
m_Item1.Explanation: The border-top shorthand property sets all the top border properties in one declaration

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTML tag is used to define an internal style sheet?
m_Item1.Explanation: <style> is used to define an internal style sheet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTML attribute is used to define inline styles?
m_Item1.Explanation: <tagname style="attributename:value;"> style attribute is used to define styles.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is the correct CSS syntax?
m_Item1.Explanation: syntax : tag name {attribute name: value}

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used as shorthand to specify a number of other font properties?
m_Item1.Explanation: font property is used to specify a number of other font properties.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to create a small-caps effect?
m_Item1.Explanation: font-variant property is used to create a small-caps effect.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to increase or decrease how bold or light a font appears?
m_Item1.Explanation: To increase or decrease boldness of font we use font-weight property.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to make a font italic or oblique?
m_Item1.Explanation: font-style property is used to make a font italic or oblique.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to change the face of a font?
m_Item1.Explanation: font-family property is used to change the face of a font.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used as a shorthand to specify a number of other background properties?
m_Item1.Explanation: background property is used to specify a number of other background properties .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct about CSS?

Options.OptionText: CSS is used to control the style of a web document in a simple and easy way.
You can write CSS once and then reuse same sheet in multiple HTML pages.
CSS allows to define style rules which can be applied to multiple tags.

m_Item1.Explanation: CSS is used to control the style of a web document in a simple and easy way. We can reuse CSS and applied to multiple tag also at the same time.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct about CSS?

Options.OptionText: Style sheets allow content to be optimized for more than one type of device.
CSS allows to separate content and style of the document.
CSS helps to reuse styles across web pages, html elements.

m_Item1.Explanation: All the statements are correct about CSS.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a component of CSS style rule?
m_Item1.Explanation: To apply style to any element we can use following component : Selector,Property,Value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which property applies a color to text?
m_Item1.Explanation: To apply color to text use only color property.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to control the scrolling of an image in the background?
m_Item1.Explanation: background-attachment property is used to control the scrolling of an image in the background.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to control the repetition of an image in the background?
m_Item1.Explanation: background-repeat property is used to control the repetition of an image in the background.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to control the position of an image in the background?
m_Item1.Explanation: background-position property is used to control the position of an image in the background.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which values for font-family property is valid?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The font-family property can hold several font names as a "fallback" system. If the browser does not support the first font, it tries the next font. There are two types of font family names.
family-name - The name of a font-family, like "times", "courier", "arial", etc.
generic-family - The name of a generic-family, like "serif", "sans-serif", "cursive", "fantasy", "monospace".
It is better practice to Start with the font you want, and always end with a generic family, to let the browser pick a similar font in the generic family, if no other fonts are available. Separate each value with a comma.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the ID selector do?

Options.OptionText: Apply the style to a specific element
Apply the style to all the elements
Apply the style to a group of elements
Apply the style to elements of the same type

m_Item1.Explanation: ID selector will apply style to a speciffic element which having specified id.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a way to associate styles with your HTML document?
m_Item1.Explanation: Two ways we can associates styles with our HTML document i.e Inline CSS or Internal CSS within <style> element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a way to associate external styles with your HTML document?

Options.OptionText: External CSS - specifying with link element

m_Item1.Explanation: Two ways we can associates external styles with our HTML document i.e External CSS using link element or using @import to import css e.g @import url("path/to/stylesheet.css");

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is true about CSS style overriding?

Options.OptionText: Any inline style sheet takes highest priority. So, it will override any rule defined in tags or rules defined in any external style sheet file.
Any rule defined in tags will override rules defined in any external style sheet file.
Any rule defined in external style sheet file takes lowest priority, and rules defined in this file will be applied only when above two rules are not applicable.

m_Item1.Explanation: For any element styles are applied using inline styles. If inline is not present, internal will be used. Even if inline is not present, external is used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following properties can be defined in terms of persent as a meaurement unit?
m_Item1.Explanation: width property of the element can be specified in terms of % width of parent element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines a measurement in centimeters?
m_Item1.Explanation: cm defines measurement in centimeters

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines a relative measurement for the height of a font in em spaces?
m_Item1.Explanation: em defines measurement for the height of a font in em spaces

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines a measurement in inches?
m_Item1.Explanation: in defines measurement in inches.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines a measurement in millimetre ?
m_Item1.Explanation: mm defines measurement in millimetre.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines a measurement in picas?
m_Item1.Explanation: pc defines measurement in piicas.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines a measurement in points?
m_Item1.Explanation: pt defines measurement in points.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines a measurement in screen pixels?
m_Item1.Explanation: px defines measurement in screen pixels

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following defines 1% of viewport height?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Example :
.content-box {  
   width: 50vw;  /* Set width to 50% of viewport width */  
   height: 75vh; /* Set height to 75% of viewport height */
}

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct about Hex Code format of CSS colors?

Options.OptionText: The first two digitsRR represent a red value.
The next two are a green valueGG.
The last are the blue valueBB.

m_Item1.Explanation:
A hex code starts with a hash symbol (#) followed by six hexadecimal digits (0-9 and A-F). he first two digits (FF) define the red intensity (255 in decimal). The next two digits (00) define the green intensity (0 in decimal).
The last two digits (00) define the blue intensity (0 in decimal). #FF0000 represents the color red.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct about Short Hex Code format of CSS colors?

Options.OptionText: This is a shorter form of the six-digit notation.
In this format, each digit is replicated to arrive at an equivalent six-digit value.
Each hexadecimal code will be preceded by hash sign '#'.

m_Item1.Explanation: When all three color channels (red, green, and blue) have the same value, you can use a shorthand notation with only three hexadecimal digits (#RGB). This repeats each digit to create the equivalent six-digit code. Standard format: #FF0000 represents the color red. Shorthand format: #F00 is equivalent to #FF0000

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct about RGB Values format of CSS colors?

Options.OptionText: This color value is specified using the rgb property.
This property takes three values, one each for red, green, and blue.
The value can be an integer between 0 and 255 or a percentage.

m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, RGB (Red, Green, Blue) values are another way to define colors. The format follows the rgb(red value, green value, blue value) function. rgb(255, 0, 0); represents the color red.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is correct about Browser Safe Colors?

Options.OptionText: These are 216 colors which are supposed to be most safe and computer independent colors.
These colors vary from hexa code 000000 to FFFFFF.
These colors are safe to use because they ensure that all computers would display the colors correctly when running a 256 color palette.

m_Item1.Explanation: Browser safe colors refer to a specific set of colors that were guaranteed to display consistently across different web browsers in the early days of the internet. Browser-safe colors are no longer a strict necessity for modern browsers. Browser-safe colors are created by combining different variations of red, green, and blue (RGB) values.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the background color of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: background-color property is used to set the background color of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following property is used to set the background image of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: background-image property is used to set the background image of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which element is used in the <HEAD> section on an HTML / XHTMLpage, if we want to use an external style sheet  file to decorate the page ?
m_Item1.Explanation: link element is used to link external style sheet to HTML page

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you insert a comment in a CSS file?
m_Item1.Explanation: To insert comment in a CSS file use /*   .....  */

m_Item1.QuestionText:
How do you display a border like this: The top border = 10 pixels, The bottom border = 5 pixels, The left
border = 20 pixels, The right border = 1pixel?
m_Item1.Explanation: Syntax : border-width: top right bottom left

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you change the left margin of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: To change left margin of an element use margin-left.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
To define the space between the element's border and content, you use the padding property, but are
you allowed to use negative values?
m_Item1.Explanation: No. we can not use padding property value as a negative.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you make a list that lists its items with squares using CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: e.g. list-style-type : square

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct CSS syntax for making all the <p> elements bold?
m_Item1.Explanation: CSS Syntax : tagname {attributename:value}

m_Item1.QuestionText: What are the three methods for using style sheets with a web page

Options.OptionText: Inline, embedded or document level and external

m_Item1.Explanation: Methods for using stylesheets are inline,embedded or document level and external.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following styles is recommended?
m_Item1.Explanation: Use of external styles is mostly recommended.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In what form are style rules presented?
m_Item1.Explanation: Styles specified in following ways : selector { property: value }

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you make the text bold?
m_Item1.Explanation: To make text bold : font-weight : bold

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTML tag is used to define an internal style sheet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The <style> tag is used within the <head> section to define internal style sheets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can we write comments in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: /* */ only can be used to add comment in CSS

m_Item1.QuestionText:
.box {
    width: 200px;
    height: 200px;
    background-color: #ff6347;
    border-radius: 50%;
    border: 2px solid #000000;
}

Which of the following describes the appearance of the .box element?

Options.OptionText: A red circular box with a black border.
A red square box with a black border.
A black circular box with a red border.
A red circular box with a red border.

m_Item1.Explanation: Border is having color #000000 means black, background color is mentioned as #ff6347 means red as border redius is applied corners will be rounded so circular box gets created

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which out of the following is valid CSS psudo class?
m_Item1.Explanation: The :before pseudo-class in CSS is used to insert content before the content of an element, which is often used in conjunction with the content property to add decorative elements or text before an element's content..

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is commonly used in CSS to hide an element visually from the layout?
m_Item1.Explanation: The display: none; property in CSS is commonly used to hide an element from the layout completely. When applied to an element, it removes it from the document flow, meaning it won't take up any space and won't be visible on the page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which selector is used for selecting all tags with a specific name in HTML?
m_Item1.Explanation:
*: Selects all elements in the document.
.class-name: Selects all elements with a specific class name.
#id-name: Selects an element with a specific ID.
tag-name: Selects all elements with a specific tag name.
The correct answer is tag-name, which is used to select all elements with a specific tag name in HTML. For example, div selects all <div> elements, p selects all <p> elements, and so on.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTML element is used to define an internal style sheet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The <style> element is used to define internal CSS styles within an HTML document.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTML attribute is used to inline CSS styles?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'style' attribute is used to apply inline CSS styles to an HTML element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you include an external CSS file in your HTML document?
m_Item1.Explanation: The <link> element is used to link an external CSS file to an HTML document.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which selector would you use to apply CSS to elements with a specific class name?
m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, a period (.) followed by the class name is used to select elements with that class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you select an element with the id 'header' in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The '#' symbol is used in CSS to select an element by its id.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which property is used to change the background color in CSS?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'background-color' property in CSS is used to set the background color of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct CSS syntax for making all the <p> elements bold?
m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, 'font-weight: bold;' is used to make text bold, and it is applied within a rule block.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS property controls the text size?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'font-size' property is used to specify the size of the text in CSS.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a valid CSS unit for specifying lengths?
m_Item1.Explanation: In CSS, 'em' is a scalable unit that is used to measure font sizes and lengths relative to the current element’s font size.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which property is used to change the font of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'font-family' property is used in CSS to specify the font of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the main purpose of the cascading effect in CSS?

Options.OptionText: To allow HTML elements to be styled differently in different browsers.
To ensure style rules are applied only when JavaScript is disabled.
To manage conflicts by prioritizing which styles should be applied.
To organize CSS files into separate categories for readability.

m_Item1.Explanation: The cascading effect in CSS determines which style rules are applied by considering factors like specificity, importance, and the order of rules.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a factor in determining the outcome of the cascading effect?
m_Item1.Explanation: Luminance is not related to the cascading effect in CSS. The factors are specificity, inheritance, and importance.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If two CSS rules apply to the same element with the same specificity, which rule is applied?

Options.OptionText: The rule that appears first in the stylesheet.
The rule that appears last in the stylesheet.
The rule with the longest selector.
The rule with the most declared properties.

m_Item1.Explanation: When two CSS rules have the same specificity, the rule that appears last in the stylesheet is applied due to the cascading nature of CSS.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which CSS rule type has the highest priority in the cascade when there is a conflict?
m_Item1.Explanation: Inline styles have the highest priority in the CSS cascade, overriding both embedded and external stylesheets, as well as browser defaults.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What role does specificity play in the cascading effect of CSS?

Options.OptionText: It ensures that less specific rules are ignored.
It dictates the overall loading time of CSS files.
It determines which rules are more important based on a numerical system.
It organizes CSS rules based on their property values.

m_Item1.Explanation: Specificity is crucial in CSS as it uses a numerical value to determine which rules are more "specific," hence more important and prioritized over others.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What are inline style sheets in web programming?

Options.OptionText: External CSS files linked from an HTML document
CSS styles defined globally for multiple pages
Styling applied directly to HTML elements using the style attribute
CSS styles defined within the head section of an HTML file

m_Item1.Explanation: Inline style sheets refer to CSS styles that are defined directly in the HTML elements using the style attribute, affecting only that specific element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which attribute are inline styles typically specified?
m_Item1.Explanation: Inline styles are specified using the style attribute in an HTML tag.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a disadvantage of using inline styles?
m_Item1.Explanation: Inline styles result in increased code redundancy and complexity because the styles need to be repeated for multiple elements instead of being centralized.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of these is an example of applying an inline style to a paragraph element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct example is using the style attribute within the paragraph tag, specifying the CSS directly in the tag.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is true about inline styles when compared to external stylesheets?

Options.OptionText: They are easier to maintain for large applications
They affect the entire document like external styles
They have higher specificity and override external stylesheets unless important is used
They load faster than external stylesheets

m_Item1.Explanation: Inline styles have higher specificity than external styles and will override them unless external styles are marked as important.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary reason for using an internal style sheet over an external style sheet?

Options.OptionText: To apply styling across multiple web pages consistently
To allow for faster loading times by not creating additional HTTP requests
To apply specific styles to a single web page without affecting others
To inherit styles from parent web pages automatically

m_Item1.Explanation: Internal style sheets are used to apply styles specifically to a single web page, which can be useful if unique styling is needed for that page without affecting styles on other pages.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Where in an HTML document is an internal style sheet typically defined?

Options.OptionText: In the <body> section of the document
Inside a <script> tag in the <head> section
Within a <style> tag in the <head> section
Within the CSS folder of the project

m_Item1.Explanation: Internal style sheets are placed within a <style> tag inside the <head> section of an HTML document, allowing the styles to be scoped to that page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you override styles defined in an external style sheet using an internal style sheet?

Options.OptionText: By re-declaring the same styles within the internal style sheet
By using the !important keyword in the external style sheet
By placing the internal style sheet before the external style sheet
By nesting the styles within a div tag in the <body>

m_Item1.Explanation: Styles defined within an internal style sheet will override those defined in an external style sheet if they are re-declared since the internal styles are closer in scope to the HTML elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following HTML tags is used to define an internal style sheet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The <style> tag is used within the <head> section to define internal CSS styles, making it specific to the particular HTML page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a potential drawback of using internal style sheets compared to external style sheets?

Options.OptionText: They require modification of each HTML file to change styles
They cause styles to be applied to too many pages

m_Item1.Explanation: Using internal style sheets requires you to modify each individual HTML file if you want to change styles, which can be inefficient compared to the centralized styling of external style sheets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTML tag is used to refer to an external style sheet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The <link> tag is used inside the head section of an HTML document to link it to an external CSS file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which attribute within the <link> tag specifies the location of the external style sheet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The href attribute in the <link> tag is used to specify the path to the external stylesheet file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the main advantage of using an external style sheet?

Options.OptionText: Allows styles to be applied inline
Enables consistent styling across multiple web pages
Requires less HTML code for each page
Loads all styles only with JavaScript

m_Item1.Explanation: External style sheets enable you to apply a consistent look and feel across multiple web pages by using the same stylesheet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following <link> tag attributes is NOT commonly associated with external style sheets?
m_Item1.Explanation: The src attribute is not generally used with the <link> tag for external stylesheets. It is typically used with script tags.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the usual media path to link an external style sheet in a HTML file?
m_Item1.Explanation: While linking an external stylesheet, it is common for web developers to store stylesheets in a directory labeled 'css', located at the root path.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which type of style sheet is defined directly within the HTML elements using the style attribute?
m_Item1.Explanation: Inline style sheets are defined directly within an HTML tag using the style attribute and affect only that particular element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is an advantage of using external style sheets?

Options.OptionText: Styles are more difficult to manage.
Only affects a single web page.
Allows applying styles to multiple pages.
They load faster than inline styles.

m_Item1.Explanation: External style sheets allow you to apply a consistent set of styles across multiple web pages by linking a single .css file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In what format are the styles applied when the style rules are included directly within the head section of an HTML document?
m_Item1.Explanation: Embedded or internal style sheets are defined within a <style> tag inside the head of an HTML document, making them effective for that document only.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you apply style rules from an external CSS file to an HTML document?

Options.OptionText: Linking the CSS file using an <img> tag.
Adding the CSS directly into the HTML tags.
Using the <link> tag within the <head> section.
Embedding the CSS file at the <body> tag.

m_Item1.Explanation: To apply styles from an external CSS file, you use the <link> tag within the <head> section of your HTML document.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When is it recommended to use inline styles in an HTML document?

Options.OptionText: When you need to apply styles to an entire website.
For consistent styling across all pages.
When you occasionally want to override styles very specifically.

m_Item1.Explanation: Inline styles are most useful for applying specific styles on individual elements when you need an occasional override that does not affect other elements or pages.
